clue: A 2011 University of Northampton study found that dairy cows have preferred partners and show a raised heart rate and higher stress when they are ___ from those best friends.
To survive dry or harsh weather, some snails can enter a dormant state and stay asleep for up to ___ years.
By slowing their heart rate, three-toed sloths can hold their breath underwater for up to ___ minutes, far longer than a dolphin.
The heart of a blue whale is so enormous that it weighs roughly ___ pounds.
The silk of Darwin's bark spider is the toughest biological material ever measured, more than ten times tougher than ___.
Axolotls can regrow lost limbs, spinal cord, and even parts of their heart and ___ without scarring.
Arctic reindeer are the only known mammal whose eyes change color with the seasons, shifting from gold in summer to ___ in winter.
When a pistol shrimp snaps its claw shut, it creates a collapsing bubble that briefly reaches a temperature roughly as hot as the surface of the ___.
Studies of prairie dog alarm calls found the animals can encode details about a predator, including its size and its ___.
In a famous mirror self-recognition test, an Asian ___ named Happy touched a mark on her own head, joining the short list of species that recognize themselves.
Because of the shape of their tail and feet, ___ cannot walk backwards, one reason they appear on Australia's coat of arms.
The traditional collective noun for a group of crows is a ___.
A baby Atlantic puffin has its own charming name: it is called a ___.
A narwhal's famous spiral tusk is not a horn at all but actually a protruding left ___ tooth.
While humans have only three color receptors in their eyes, the mantis shrimp has as many as ___.
In a zero-oxygen atmosphere, a naked mole-rat can survive about ___ minutes apparently without harm.
